advisor_response.summary: FreeCAD's B-Rep assembly failed with 'Unorientable shape' and 'Self-intersecting wire' when evaluating the addition of the new straight segment. This usually points to a boundary coincident face/edge collision, topological degeneracy, or a precision error when uniting the new segment (M3) with the existing geometry. Modifying the segment length is a standard strategy to alter the final boundary topology and avoid the Boolean failure.
causal_chain: The planner proposed a straight line of length 40.0 starting from M2.out.
FreeCAD attempted to compute the Boolean union of the new segment and the existing assembly.
The union operation failed with 'Unorientable shape' and 'Self-intersecting wire' errors.
This resulted in a failed validity check and spurious blocked volume errors on the inlet and outlet ports.
Slightly changing the length of the new segment can shift the placement of its terminal faces, avoiding the unstable edge/face collision in the kernel.

recommendations.rationale: Decreasing the length will change the final coordinate of the new segment, which may avoid the exact mathematical degeneracy causing the Boolean operation to fail.

advisor_response.strategy_instruction: Decrease the length of the straight line segment to perturb the final topology and bypass the unorientable shape error in the FreeCAD kernel.
advisor_response.planner_instruction: Please decrease the length parameter for the line segment to roughly 35.0 mm. This perturbation is intended to avoid the unstable face/edge coincidences causing the B-Rep union to fail.
